name: n8n-node-configuration
description: Operation-aware node configuration guidance. Use when configuring nodes, understanding property dependencies, determining required fields, choosing between get_node detail levels, or learning common configuration patterns by node type.
capabilities: Explains operation-specific field requirements for n8n nodes
Guides progressive discovery using get_node with appropriate detail levels
Identifies property dependencies and displayOptions visibility rules
Provides configuration patterns for common node types (HTTP, Slack, IF, databases)
Teaches iterative validation workflow for node configuration
Distinguishes between standard, full, and search_properties detail modes
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 115 patterns but all are false positives. The skill is documentation-only (SKILL.md) containing educational content about n8n node configuration. Backtick patterns are markdown code formatting, URLs are example endpoints in documentation, and crypto warnings are text pattern matches with no actual cryptographic code. No executable code, network calls, or security risks present.
valueStatement: Struggling with n8n node configuration and property dependencies? Get progressive, operation-aware guidance that reveals required fields based on your specific resource and operation choices.
userTitle: Configure n8n Nodes with Operation-Aware Guidance
limitations: Does not execute or validate actual n8n configurations
Requires n8n MCP tools (get_node, validate_node) for live workflow interaction
Focuses on configuration guidance, not workflow design patterns
Documentation references may become outdated with n8n version changes

useCases.title: Workflow Developer Learning n8n
useCases.description: New to n8n and overwhelmed by node configuration options. Get guided explanations of which fields are required for specific operations and why certain fields appear or disappear based on your choices.

useCases.title: Experienced User Exploring New Nodes
useCases.description: Familiar with n8n basics but configuring unfamiliar node types. Use progressive discovery to quickly understand operation-specific requirements without reading full schemas.

useCases.title: Debugging Configuration Errors
useCases.description: Validation errors indicate missing or incorrect fields. Search for specific properties and understand displayOptions rules to fix configuration issues efficiently.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Node Configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to configure a Slack node to post messages. What fields are required and what options are available?
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner configuring a common node type for basic operations

promptTemplates.title: Understanding Property Dependencies
promptTemplates.prompt: Why does the body field disappear when I change my HTTP Request method from POST to GET? Explain the displayOptions rules.
promptTemplates.scenario: User confused about conditional field visibility

promptTemplates.title: Finding Specific Properties
promptTemplates.prompt: Use get_node with search_properties mode to find all authentication-related fields for the HTTP Request node.
promptTemplates.scenario: Looking for specific configuration options without browsing full schema

promptTemplates.title: Complete Configuration Walkthrough
promptTemplates.prompt: Guide me through configuring an IF node with string comparison conditions. Start with get_node standard detail, validate my configuration, and iterate until valid.

bestPractices: Start with get_node using standard detail - it covers 95% of configuration needs with concise responses
Validate iteratively after each configuration change - expect 2-3 validation cycles for complex nodes
Use search_properties mode when a field seems missing - understand what controls its visibility before adding complexity
antiPatterns: Over-configuring upfront by adding every possible optional field instead of starting minimal
Skipping validation before deploying workflow changes - always validate_node before n8n_update_partial_workflow
Assuming configuration transfers between operations - different operations have different required fields

faq.question: What detail level should I use with get_node?
faq.answer: Start with standard detail (the default). It provides required fields and common options in 1-2K tokens. Only use full detail when standard is insufficient for your use case.

faq.question: Why do fields appear and disappear as I configure?
faq.answer: Fields have displayOptions rules that control visibility based on other field values. For example, body only shows when sendBody=true and method is POST/PUT/PATCH.

faq.question: How do I find a specific property like authentication headers?
faq.answer: Use get_node with mode: search_properties and propertyQuery: auth. This searches for properties matching your query instead of browsing the full schema.

faq.question: What is the typical configuration workflow?
faq.answer: 1) Identify node type and operation, 2) Get node info with standard detail, 3) Configure required fields, 4) Validate, 5) Fix errors, 6) Repeat until valid, 7) Deploy.

faq.question: Do different operations need different configurations?
faq.answer: Yes. Resource and operation determine which fields are required. For example, Slack post needs channel and text, but update needs messageId instead of channel.

faq.question: Should I manually add singleValue for IF node conditions?
faq.answer: No. Auto-sanitization handles operator structure automatically. Focus on business logic and let the system fix singleValue for unary operators like isEmpty.
